title: -Contract: Preferred Alliance, Inc. for Drug and Alcohol Testing Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to increase the amount of the current
contract and any amendments with Preferred Alliance Inc., to continue to provide third
party administration of the City’s Random Drug and Alcohol Testing Program which
expires at the end of 2025. An extension is necessary through June 30, 2027. A
contract cap increase is required in an amount not to exceed $100,000 to continue
services.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The cost for third-party administrative services provided by Preferred Alliance, Inc. is
expected to total approximately $25,000 per fiscal year. An increase of $51,000 is
required for FY 2026 - FY 2027. Expenditures for these services are paid from a variety
of funding sources in budgets of individual operating departments. Primary users of this
service are Public Works, Health Housing and Community Services, Parks, Recreation
and Waterfront departments.
sections.current_situation:
In order to continue drug and alcohol testing of safety-sensitive transportation
employees an increase in contract allocation is necessary. The current contract is
capped at $49,000 which has been expended through services from 2022-2025.
Additional funds are needed to cover the remainder of 2025 through June 30, 2027.
sections.background:
The Omnibus Transportation Employee Testing Act of 1991 requires drug and alcohol
testing of safety-sensitive transportation employees. The Federal Department of
Transportation (DOT) and the Federal Motor Carriers Safety Administration (FMCSA)
publishes rules under the Code of Federal Regulations (CFR 49Part 382 and 49 CFR
Part 40 respectively). The City employs approximately 185 federally regulated
commercial drivers and is required to effectively conduct a Drug and Alcohol random
selection testing program that is compliant with regulatory requirements. The rules
specifically require the City to conduct pre-employment, reasonable suspicion, random
selection, post-accident, return to duty, follow up drug testing, and supervisor training.

E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 3
Preferred Alliance Contract Amendment CONSENT CALENDAR
October 14, 2025
As the City’s current drug and alcohol testing third-party administrator, Preferred
Alliance, Inc. has performed very well and has been responsive to the needs of the City.
They continue to provide the City with outstanding technical and administrative support
including training and certification of the City’s supervisors as well as interpreting
regulations to develop policy updates and procedures that meet regulatory
requirements.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects or opportunities associated with the
subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
The additional funding is necessary to continue administrating the City’s drug and
alcohol testing program (A.R. 2.13). The City will benefit by meeting our regulatory
obligations and maintain the ability to carry out administrative regulation A.R.2.13.
Additionally, the increase in funding will keep key interdepartmental functions ongoing
i.e., new hire process, for employee classifications that require a commercial drivers’
license and ensure that multiple operational processes are not interrupted or negatively
impacted.
sections.alternatives:
A Request for Proposals is underway.
CONTACT PERSON
Janelle Rodrigues, Director of Human Resources 510-981-6807
Kevin Walker, Safety Officer 510-981-6825
sections.attachments:
Resolution
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
Third Party Administration of Department of Transportation Drug and Alcohol Testing
WHEREAS, the Department of Transportation (DOT) and the Federal Motor Carrier
Safety Administration (FMCSA) requires the City to conduct drug and alcohol testing of
safety-sensitive transportation employees; and
WHEREAS, Article XI, Section 67.5 of the Charter of the City of Berkeley and Section
7.18.010 of the Berkeley Municipal Code requires City Council approval of contracts that
exceed $50,000; and
WHEREAS, the City entered into a Contract No. 32400109 in the amount not to exceed
$49,000 with Preferred Alliance Inc. to provide third party administration; and
WHEREAS, it is important that third party services continue without negative impacts to
internal operations and support of the ongoing drug and alcohol program covered under
the Federal Department of Transportation Guidelines; and
NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager, or designee, is authorized to execute an increase in funding for Contract
No. 32400109 with Preferred Alliance Inc. to increase the contract cap for a total not to
exceed $100,000. Expenses to be paid by departments utilizing the services from a
variety of funding sources in budgets previously adopted by the departments of Public
Works; Health Housing and Community Service; and Parks, Recreation and Waterfront.
